codeReview.summary: The feature commit adds only characterization coverage in tests/coin-detail-parity.test.ts. I found one high-confidence blocking issue in that new coverage: the degraded seeded-bootstrap assertion contradicts the shared runtime policy and the diagnostics contract already codified elsewhere in the mission. The route implementation itself was not changed by this feature commit.

issues.description: The new test expects diagnostics to report `degraded.stale_live_enabled: true` for `degraded_seeded_bootstrap`, but the shared access policy intentionally treats that mode as bootstrap-only access with `allowStaleLiveService: false` (`src/modules/market-freshness.ts:105-110`) and the dedicated diagnostics suite already locks the public contract to `stale_live_enabled === false` for that mode (`tests/runtime-diagnostics.test.ts:345`). This feature therefore bakes in the previously identified wrong truth and would either fail against the correct implementation or pressure later changes to regress diagnostics semantics.

summary: Fail. The feature only adds tests, but one new assertion encodes the wrong degraded-seeded-bootstrap diagnostics semantics (`stale_live_enabled` should be false, not true), so the review cannot pass as written.
